entries.title: Phillies' Kyle Schwarber closing in on Babe Ruth's MLB record
entries.description: Schwarber leads MLB in home runs so far this season.

entries.title: Argentina’s World Cup comeback
entries.description: Lionel Messi and Argentina defeated Egypt today after trailing behind them for much of the game. The team scored three goals in under 15 minutes. NBC News’ Jesse Kirsch has the story.

entries.title: Southern Poverty Law Center pleads not guilty in federal fraud case
entries.description: The Southern Poverty Law Center, best known for monitoring hate groups, pleaded not guilty Tuesday to federal charges that it defrauded donors by not disclosing that some of the money would be used to pay informants in extremist organizations.

entries.title: U.S. launches strikes against Iran over attacks on 3 ships in Strait of Hormuz
entries.description: The Islamic Revolutionary Guard Corps has over the past two days attacked three commercial vessels that were traveling through the Strait of Hormuz.

entries.title: License of Utah school where Paris Hilton alleged she was abused is revoked
entries.description: Utah has revoked the license of a boarding school where Paris Hilton said she was abused as a teenager, saying the school has "failed to provide applicable health and safety services for clients."

entries.title: Here's how Democrats could pick a new Maine Senate nominee if Platner drops out
entries.description: Democrats have a brief window of time to replace Maine Senate candidate Graham Platner on the ballot if he drops out of the race amid a sexual assault allegation.

entries.title: Unstable building in Midtown forces evacuations, street closures

entries.title: YouTube defends video that falsely claims Sydney massacre survivor is 'crisis actor'

entries.title: U.S. says China's nuclear weapons buildup "of great concern" after missile test

entries.description: In a strategic move, VVS Laxman will lead India's white-ball team during the Zimbabwe tour and Asian Games, while Gautam Gambhir steps back after the England series. Anticipated to assist Laxman are Hrishikesh Kanitkar and Sunil Joshi. This coaching arrangement reflects India's effort to navigate a tightly-packed tournament schedule in international cricket.

entries.title: Boomer Parents: It's Time to Cough it Up | Money Moves with Jill Schlesinger
entries.description:
Americans have been anxiously awaiting the so-called "great wealth transfer," where trillions of dollars are expected to pass down from Baby Boomers to their kids. But people are living longer and holding onto their assets. Jill says it's time to start opening up the purse strings now. Plus, caller questions: Toby asks whether sterling silver jewelry is actually a worthwhile investment, and Dave wants to know if taking a pay cut to improve his quality of life is financially realistic.

Have a money question? Email askjill@jillonmoney.com.

entries.title: Trump says he's not sure he wants to make a deal with Iran
entries.description: President Trump on Wednesday said he wasn't sure he wants to make a deal with Iran and threatened more strikes against the country. CBS News' Nancy Cordes reports and Robert B. Murrett, professor of practice at Syracuse University, joins with analysis.

entries.title: Sneak peek: My Mother's Murder Trials
entries.description: ENCORE: A daughter suspects her mother of a double murder. Years later, courtroom drama brings unsettling answers. CBS News chief correspondent Jim Axelrod reports in a two-part "48 Hours" airing Saturday, July 11 from 9-11 p.m. ET on CBS and streaming on Paramount+.

entries.title: 2026 Emmy nominations are announced: See the full list

entries.title: Former Wisconsin judge spared prison for obstructing ICE arrest of Mexican immigrant
entries.description: Former Wisconsin Judge Hannah Dugan was spared from prison Wednesday for ushering a Mexican defendant out of her courtroom to evade U.S. Immigration and Customs Enforcement agents. A federal judge fined her $5,000 and cited her otherwise law-abiding life in issuing the sentence.

entries.title: Stocks slide, oil surges after Trump says Iran ceasefire is over
entries.description: Oil prices jumped 6% while U.S. stocks fell as renewed Middle East conflict threatens crude shipments through the Strait of Hormuz.

entries.title: Two teens detained in California after drinking, shooting toy guns out of Waymo robotaxi
entries.description: Waymo halted one of its robotaxis mid-ride and called California cops after it caught two teen passengers drinking booze and shooting pellets from toy guns, police said.

entries.title: Strait of Hormuz traffic won't return to normal until 2027 after latest setback, Kalshi traders predict

entries.title: Family demands an independent probe after ICE officer fatally shoots a man in Houston
entries.description: A Mexican national fatally shot by an Immigration and Customs Enforcement officer in Houston had no criminal convictions during his decades living in the U.S. and was driving a crew to a homebuilding site when he was killed, his family and a Texas congresswoman said Wednesday.

entries.title: What to know about protecting pets from the New World screwworm fly
entries.description: Two New World screwworm cases in dogs are among more than 30 confirmed instances in Texas and New Mexico, prompting warnings Wednesday from veterinarians and humane societies that pet owners need to remain vigilant to protect their animals.

entries.title: The Trump administration is ramping up pressure on states to change election practices

entries.title: Canada's Carney visits Saudi Arabia as the prime minister seeks to expand ties with kingdom
entries.description: Canada's Prime Minister Mark Carney arrived in Saudi Arabia on Wednesday - the first visit to the kingdom by a Canadian leader in 26 years - as he seeks to expand his country's economic ties beyond its heavy reliance on the United States.
